name: NumberInput
description: Numeric input with stepper buttons and locale formatting.

api.name: NumberInput
api.description: Numeric input with stepper buttons and locale formatting.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The current numeric value; null when empty.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Two-way callback for Value.

parameters.name: Min
parameters.description: Lower bound; typed/stepped values are clamped to it. Null means no lower bound.

parameters.name: Max
parameters.description: Upper bound; typed/stepped values are clamped to it. Null means no upper bound.

parameters.name: Step
parameters.description: Amount added/subtracted per +/- click, ArrowUp/ArrowDown, or mouse-wheel tick. Default 1.

parameters.name: Precision
parameters.description: Number of decimal places the value is rounded to on every change. Null leaves fractional precision unrounded (except for FP cleanup on stepped values).

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: Disables the input and both stepper buttons. Default false.

parameters.name: UseMouseWheel
parameters.description: Enables incrementing/decrementing by scrolling the mouse wheel over the input. Default false.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: Text rendered inside the input before the value (e.g. a currency symbol).

parameters.name: Suffix
parameters.description: Text rendered inside the input after the value (e.g. a unit).

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Culture used to parse typed input. Display of the value inside the native <input type="number"> element uses InvariantCulture because HTML5 requires period-as-decimal — browsers will not accept a comma decimal in this input type. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization for parsing (lets a de-DE user paste 1,5 and have it round-trip to 1.5).

parameters.name: Required
parameters.description: Marks the field as required, rendering a trailing asterisk on the label and setting aria-required. Default false.

parameters.name: Invalid
parameters.description: Forces the invalid (error) visual state and aria-invalid, independent of any ancestor FormField. Default false.

parameters.name: ErrorText
parameters.description: Error text shown below the control when invalid (outside a FormField) and not inside a FormField.

parameters.name: HelperText
parameters.description: Helper text shown below the control when not invalid and not inside a FormField.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text rendered above the control (unless the control is inside a FormField, which renders its own label).

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Explicit accessible name for the input, taking priority over Label when both are set.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name attribute for form submission.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the underlying number <input> on first render. See Input.AutoFocus for why the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-control density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the control height shifts between values.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the control's outer wrapper.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Unmatched attributes splatted onto the control's outer wrapper.

parameters.name: Block
parameters.description: Stretch the control to fill its container — a full-width input between the −/+ buttons — instead of sizing to its content. Use it in form layouts so the field lines up with sibling full-width inputs/selects (the LumeoForm generator sets it).

mdSummary:
# NumberInput

Numeric input with stepper buttons and locale formatting.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Forms/Inputs
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/number-input

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NumberInput | FormField | `FormField.FormFieldContext?` | `` |  |
| NumberInput | Value | `double?` | `` | The current numeric value; null when empty. |
| NumberInput | ValueChanged | `EventCallback<double?>` | `` | Two-way callback for Value. |
| NumberInput | Min | `double?` | `` | Lower bound; typed/stepped values are clamped to it. Null means no lower bound. |
| NumberInput | Max | `double?` | `` | Upper bound; typed/stepped values are clamped to it. Null means no upper bound. |
| NumberInput | Step | `double` | `1` | Amount added/subtracted per +/- click, ArrowUp/ArrowDown, or mouse-wheel tick. Default 1. |
| NumberInput | Precision | `int?` | `` | Number of decimal places the value is rounded to on every change. Null leaves fractional precision unrounded (except for FP cleanup on stepped values). |
| NumberInput | Disabled | `bool` | `false` | Disables the input and both stepper buttons. Default false. |
| NumberInput | UseMouseWheel | `bool` | `false` | Enables incrementing/decrementing by scrolling the mouse wheel over the input. Default false. |
| NumberInput | Prefix | `string?` | `` | Text rendered inside the input before the value (e.g. a currency symbol). |
| NumberInput | Suffix | `string?` | `` | Text rendered inside the input after the value (e.g. a unit). |
| NumberInput | Culture | `System.Globalization.CultureInfo?` | `` | Culture used to parse typed input. Display of the value inside the native <input type="number"> element uses InvariantCulture because HTML5 requires period-as-decimal — browsers will not accept a comma decimal in this input type. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization for parsing (lets a de-DE user paste 1,5 and have it round-trip to 1.5). |
| NumberInput | Required | `bool` | `false` | Marks the field as required, rendering a trailing asterisk on the label and setting aria-required. Default false. |
| NumberInput | Invalid | `bool` | `false` | Forces the invalid (error) visual state and aria-invalid, independent of any ancestor FormField. Default false. |
| NumberInput | ErrorText | `string?` | `` | Error text shown below the control when invalid (outside a FormField) and not inside a FormField. |
| NumberInput | HelperText | `string?` | `` | Helper text shown below the control when not invalid and not inside a FormField. |
| NumberInput | Label | `string?` | `` | Label text rendered above the control (unless the control is inside a FormField, which renders its own label). |
| NumberInput | AriaLabel | `string?` | `` | Explicit accessible name for the input, taking priority over Label when both are set. |
| NumberInput | Name | `string?` | `` | The name attribute for form submission. |
| NumberInput | AutoFocus | `bool` | `false` | Focuses the underlying number <input> on first render. See Input.AutoFocus for why the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM. |
| NumberInput | Density | `Density?` | `` | Per-control density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the control height shifts between values. |
| NumberInput | InheritedDensity | `Density?` | `` |  |
| NumberInput | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the control's outer wrapper. |
| NumberInput | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Unmatched attributes splatted onto the control's outer wrapper. |
| NumberInput | Block | `bool` | `false` | Stretch the control to fill its container — a full-width input between the −/+ buttons — instead of sizing to its content. Use it in form layouts so the field lines up with sibling full-width inputs/selects (the LumeoForm generator sets it). |

## Slots
- (none)

## Events
- `ValueChanged` (`EventCallback<double?>`) — Two-way callback for Value.

## Sub-components
- (none)

## Cascading values it expects
- `FormField` (`FormField.FormFieldContext?`)
- `InheritedDensity` (`Density?`)

## Services it injects
- `ILumeoLocalizer` (Lumeo.Services.Localization)

## Keyboard
—

## Example
```razor
<NumberInput />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-background`, `--color-border`, `--color-destructive`, `--color-foreground`, `--color-muted`, `--color-muted-foreground`, `--color-ring`, `--radius`
